chunks.text: The human brain weighs about 1.
3 kilograms.
Though only a tiny part of the body in weight
inside it lies a network of 86 billion neuron.
Each neuron is linked with thousands of others through a synapse.
In the whole brain, around 100 trillion synapses form a netlike network.
Memory, perception, emotion, judgment.
Everything we call " self " arises from this complex neural activity.
How consciousness rises from the matter of the brain — this is one of the greatest mysteries of science.
Since the late twentieth century
imaging technology such as functional MRI has allowed researchers to directly observe a brain in action.
What part of the brain becomes active when we see something
when we remember something — these can now be tracked in real time.
One recent discovery is the plasticity of the brain.
Once, it had been believed that the adult brain no longer changed.
But it is now known that the connections between neurons continue to change throughout life through new experience and learning.
Alzheimer, Parkinson, stroke.
Treatments for those who suffer brain disease and brain injury are still being studied around the world.
To draw out, to its fullest, the power of the brain's recovery.
Brain research leads us toward the deepest question we may ever ask: what is a human being?
